rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND M. AKASHI
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although this report of status appears to be identical to the first report of status, which indicates that no progress has been made on the estate administration or valuation of estate assets. There is reportedly cash on hand but no I&A has been filed yet to show the cash assets that have been marshaled. The executor must file either a petition for final distribution or a third petition for approval of a report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 8/25/25. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PETER Q. YANG TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Trust Administration And Appointment Of Yijun Yang As Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/18/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice was not given to the attorneys-in-fact for Rose Yang Chow and Helen Yang. (2) Did Sam Y.C. Yang decline to serve, or resign from serving, as the attorney-in-fact for Helen Yu Yang? (3) Notice is defective if mailed before the pleading requiring notice is e-filed. [LRSR 14.14 A.] (4) What happened to Loa Rose Chun? Courtesy copies were not received by the assigned examiner by the 4/28/25 deadline or even as of 5/7/25, but they were skimmed online in hopes of avoiding another continuance. Receipt of courtesy copies alerts the assigned examiner that responsive papers have been filed; otherwise, review can be delayed or the hearing simply continued as of the deadline. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Deadline is 6/4/25 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies, including of (1) a second supplement to the petition (to address the missing beneficiary and to set forth the addresses of the attorneys-in-fact and explain why the first named attorney-in-fact is not serving as Helen Yu Yang's agent) and (2) a proof of service of 30 days' notice of the 6/18/25 hearing of the petition and amendment to all interested parties.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ENEZ ANITA BESSARD TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Beneficiaries Petition For An Order Directing Successor Trustee, Gwendolyn Sacco To Purchase Each Beneficiary'S Interest Or To Sell Trust Realty
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/2/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. (2) What is Jasmine Coussinat's standing? She does not appear to be a beneficiary. (3) Is Cynthia Estrada Estrada (?) the daughter named in the trust as Cynthia Bessard? (4) Item 2 of the proof of service filed on 3/11/25 is incomplete. (5) A copy of the petition was not provided [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.], so it has only been cursorily reviewed, but it does not appear that all interested parties (other trust beneficiaries) received notice. (6) Notice must be given directly to a person entitled to notice, not in care of another. If a party entitled to notice is represented by an attorney, notice must also be given to the attorney, but not only to the attorney. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b)]. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/18/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL TREFZGER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/23/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The copy of the will that is attached to the petition is not the same document as the original that was filed/lodged. (2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Who was/were the sibling(s) who was/were the parent(s) of the niblings? (3) Herbert J. Trefzger, the brother and alternate executor named in the will, is missing from item 8 of the petition (4) Item 8 must clearly identify the trustee of the devisee trust and, if the petitioner is the trustee, identify all of the trust beneficiaries. (5) Did the testator leave a separate Personal Property Memorandum? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/9/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOLITA C. ARCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/30/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of publication of notice has been filed. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be answered. (4) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the parents of the two grandchildren and when did they die? (5) An amendment was just filed on 4/24/25, but no notice of it appears to have been given. [CRC 7.53(a).] Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and supporting papers, including any papers filed to address these issues, to the assigned examiner by 6/16/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Items 6 and 7 of a petition for probate/letters of administration do not need to be completed when a decedent is survived by issue.)
